module_mood_contract: earned trust — 'the way I work with agents is something my staff engineer and my CISO can sign off on, before I even try anything big'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honored; mood-floor applies to teaching beats only.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block names mood target explicitly: 'earned trust — the way I work with agents is something my staff engineer and my CISO can sign off on, before I even try anything big' (L100). Teaching beats: P2=8, P4=8, P5=9, P6=8, P7=9, P9=8. Setup beats P1/P3/P8 correctly mood-null per carve-out (prework feature pick, two-window launch, session-close transition — each compressible to a parenthetical without breaking the arc).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood, not punitive.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea (L6) leads with discipline ('earn your staff engineer's and CISO's trust', 'curated skills do the security work, one skill you author does the quality work'). No shame/failure framing. ## Bring to Module 4 stakes line (L85) is forward-tension ('Your call.'), not punitive.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Title 'Earn the trust' names positive discipline. Big Idea opens with 'earn your staff engineer's and CISO's trust' — discipline-lead. Key Concepts (L43-49) lead with discipline verbs (invoke, ask, ship); breadcrumbs name moves (invoke-don't-re-derive, your-codebase-teaches-the-skill).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — doesn't front-run downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc M3 in-detail names: first Skill use, first subagent, first authoring all land at M3. File honors this: Ex1/Ex2 invoke curated skills as subagents (L20-21), Ex3 authoring in main thread (L23). M4 hand-off (L77) names what M4 will use ('the access-surface facts, and the hardening decision all feed into what M4 builds') without revealing M4 teaching shape. M5 send-off / M6 second-skill stay sealed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts (L43-49) instance AE101 themes: 'invoke, don't re-derive' = compounding-through-curation; 'mirror reflects what your own read would miss' = mechanically self-aware (#4); 'Authoring without invocation is theatre' = capture-your-world + non-agentic-default contrast; 'team kit grows by accretion' = Klaassen compounding. Two or three themes instanced per the rule.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts breadcrumbs — variety beats consistency, no verbatim adjacent repetition.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Two breadcrumbs (L43 'invoke, don't re-derive' and L46 'your codebase teaches the skill, not the manual'). Adjacent module M2 (plan-mode-done-right) breadcrumbs: '10% wrong' + 'push-backs ship into memory' — different themes, no verbatim overlap with M3. Within the 1-3 ceiling.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: What You'll Learn — capability register, engineer voice, no apparatus.
rules_evaluated.evidence: L20-26: every bullet leads with a concrete verb (Invoke / Apply / Split / Author / Test / Invoke / Ship) and names a real artifact (curated skill, ADR, subagent, test-strategy skill, security-tested feature). No Bloom tags, no apparatus tails, no author-defence asides. Engineer-to-peer register.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lecture placement — vocabulary mid-module, meta-frame as closer.
rules_evaluated.evidence: L34: 'Lecture: Skills from the frontier, skills of your own' lands AFTER Connections + Open-the-side-quest, BEFORE the curated-skill invocations. Vocabulary lecture at the exact moment the vocab earns itself — 'curated skill', 'subagent', 'main thread'. Per §1, mid-module placement for vocabulary is correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M3 is earned-trust, not contrast-mood (M5 is the contrast-mood arc per strategy doc). Rule does not fire.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Each phase's forcing function fenced inside a {{prompt:...}}: ae101-m3-sharpen-skill (L59), ae101-m3-ready-to-clear (L67), ae101-m3-clear-session (L73). Body callouts (L61 opt-up, L57 m3-quality-still-open) explain mechanism but do not carry the load-bearing teaching beat — those live in the prompts. Speed-runner reading prompt-to-prompt still hits the sharpen + close.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room — no authored conversations in body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Connections opener (L30) carries the harvest question per the 2026-05-03 carve-out ('The room's features will differ wildly; that's the point.') — in-scope-by-design. No 'share with the room', 'discuss with neighbor', 'tell the room' phrases anywhere. Body talks about WORK (feature pick, surface naming, ADR write, skill author, sharpen, clear).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block L133-136 names three falsifiable Monday outcomes: (1) invokes curated security skill on a real PR before review; (2) writes an ADR for one architectural decision under named pressure; (3) invokes the authored test-strategy skill on a feature without being prompted. Each falsifiable via PR comment / new ADR file at sponsor path / scrollback grep.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch table.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block L146-155 carries the table — six rows, each forcing function paired with dominant failure mode + escape hatch. Pedagogy judge would block ship on missing rows; all phases shipping forcing functions have entries.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Multi-session ships as packaging, not new primitive.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Two-window mechanic shipped as packaging via open-the-side-quest.md (setup exercise) + per-section **Window:** *m3-security* callouts (L53). M3's three taught primitives (first Skill use, first subagent, first authoring) stay budgeted; the multi-session container teaches by living, not by lecturing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: /clear is a tool with a purpose, not a ceremony.
rules_evaluated.evidence: L65-73 fires the convergence (ae101-m3-ready-to-clear) before /clear (ae101-m3-clear-session) in m3-security because both purposes apply (compounds may earn themselves; M4 opens a new session). m3-quality close is the student's call — no scripted /clear in the body for that window. Both-question diagnostic landed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Task-selection floor AND ceiling explicitly named.
rules_evaluated.evidence: L12 prework names both floor and ceiling: 'Not a typo-fix, not a quarter-long epic. Something you could ship in a few hours. Too small and the agent crunches it in thirty seconds with nothing interesting to surface. Too large and you don't get through it.' ## Bring to Module 4 (L83) restates for M4: 'Not an epic. Not a typo-fix. A real slice with a "done" you can name in a sentence.' Anti-pattern callout present in both pre-think and execution-commit positions.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — next module's teaching moment intact.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M3 closes in earned-trust register (mood 8-9 per trace close). M4's mood per strategy doc is 'curious readiness then deepened unease' (walk-and-send-off). ## Next (L77) frames forward-look as 'memory that reads your system, not just a feature' — promises a turn without revealing the un-packaged-send-off teaching moment. The hardening decision and test-strategy skill feed M4's gap analysis but M3 doesn't pre-teach gap analysis.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — earned trust holds across skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Trace P5 mood 9: ADR write lands as 'a real architectural call under named pressure, not a compliance checkbox.' Trace P6 mood 8: authored test-strategy skill = codebase-tuned. Trace P7 mood 9: sharpen-from-evidence with m3-quality-window cross-reference makes the evidence trail real. The earned-trust contract — defensibility before staff engineer + CISO — fires three times (ADR, authored skill, sharpened skill) and survives persona-skill variation (no single move carries the whole load).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file honors the strategy-doc framing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc M3 Big Idea: curated-then-authored — curated skills for security (Ex1+Ex2), authored skill for quality (Ex3), team kit seeded. File Big Idea (L6) matches: 'Two curated skills from the frontier do the security work, one skill you author through conversation with Claude does the quality work. Team kit accretion starts here: the skill you author this hour ships personal first, with a clear path to team promotion through a human conversation later.' Curated-then-authored proportion + team-kit-seed + personal-first-with-team-promotion-via-conversation all present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off to next open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: L77 ## Next: 'Two signatures, earned. Your staff engineer sees a test-strategy skill tuned to this codebase, your CISO sees a STRIDE decision with an ADR. Module 4 turns the discipline inward: memory that reads your system, not just a feature.' Hands the ADR + test-strategy skill forward as M4 inputs (matches strategy doc M4 'Walk what you've built' — Claude audits root CLAUDE.md + memory + ADRs + M3 test-strategy skill). M2→M3 plan-continues default in prework (L12) restores the M2→M3 hand-off shape that the 2026-05-22 cohort suggestion surfaced as cadence-shaped.
